item.title: Bob Dylan
item.description: Genres: folk rock, contemporary folk, singer-songwriter, blues rock, country rock Scene: Greenwich Village folk scene Era: 1961–present (primary period: 1962–1976) RYM · Apple Music · Spotify Bob Dylan arrived in New York in January 1961 as a nineteen-year-old from Hibbing, Minnesota, carrying an ac...

item.title: Chuck Berry
item.description: Genres: Rock & roll, rhythm & blues Scene: Chicago (Chess Records) Era: 1955–1964 (primary period) RYM · Apple Music · Spotify The intro to “Johnny B.

item.title: Muddy Waters
item.description: Genres: Chicago blues, Electric blues, Delta blues Scene: Chicago (Chess Records) Era: 1948–1958 (Chess peak); active to 1983 RYM · Apple Music · Spotify A bottleneck slide rings out through a cranked amplifier, the note bending and sustaining far past anything an acoustic guitar could hold, and a v...

item.title: The Rolling Stones
item.description: Genres: British blues, blues rock, rock & roll, roots rock Scene: London (Richmond, then Soho) Era: 1962–present (primary period: 1968–1972) RYM · Apple Music · Spotify Keith Richards plays behind the beat.

item.title: Authenticity and its discontents
item.description: Simon Frith’s Performing Rites makes a claim that sounds simple and proves difficult to absorb: when a listener calls a record “authentic,” they are not detecting a quality the music possesses but applying a set of learned conventions about what authentic music should sound like.

item.title: The pop factory
item.description: Theodor Adorno argued in On Popular Music that the culture industry standardizes its products: every hit follows a pre-given pattern, and the variations between individual songs are surface differences designed to mask that uniformity.

item.title: The transatlantic feedback loop
item.description: The pattern that defines the transatlantic movement of popular music — Black American musicians creating a form, white British musicians absorbing and re-exporting it, America responding to the British version — has repeated across so many decades that it functions as a permanent circuit.

item.title: Chess Records
item.description: Founded: 1950 (as Chess Records; predecessor Aristocrat Records from 1947) Location: Chicago, Illinois (2120 South Michigan Avenue, 1957–1967) Founders: Leonard Chess, Phil Chess Key years: 1950–1969 Subsidiaries: Checker Records (1952), Argo Records (1955; renamed Cadet, 1965) RYM The sound of 2120...

item.title: Blues
item.description: Period: c. 1900–present Geographic origin: African American communities of the U.S.
